name: Drupal Arbitrary Code Execution Detected
description: Detects exploitation attempts targeting the Drupalgeddon2 vulnerability (CVE-2018-7600), which allows for arbitrary code execution. This rule identifies suspicious POST requests to specific paths associated with the vulnerability.

prompts.prompt: Attempted exploitation of Drupalgeddon2 (CVE-2018-7600) detected, indicating possible arbitrary code execution. Event Details: **Timestamp** {Timestamp}, **Source Address** {source_address}, **Destination Address** {destination_address}, **User** {user}, **URL** {url}, **Request Method** {request_method}, **User Agent** {user_agent}. Investigate this critical event immediately for signs of compromise.

parameters.name: Timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the web access event.

parameters.name: source_address
parameters.description: The source IP address of the request.

parameters.name: destination_address
parameters.description: The destination IP address of the web server.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user accessing the URL, if available.

parameters.name: user_agent
parameters.description: The User-Agent header from the request.
